commit.message:
Add Generate-FlowBody.ps1: builds PA HTTP body from form questions

Interactive script that:
- Extracts form ID from URL
- Asks for question count and titles
- Outputs complete HTTP action config (URI, headers, body JSON)
  with correct Power Automate expressions pre-filled
- Saves body to power-automate-body.json for easy copy-paste

Eliminates manual question-ID mapping (r1, r2, r3...).
Setup guide Step 4.3 updated to reference the script.

commit.message:
fix(ci): typos config + analysis test assertions (#570)

## Summary

Fixes three CI failures:

### 1. Typos check
Added \strat\ and \donn\ to \_typos.toml\ extend-words to suppress false
positives from proptest strategy function names (e.g. \
ile_ext_strat()\) and French word \données\ in Unicode test fixtures.

### 2. Analysis test assertions
Fixed three failing tests in \nalysis_depth_w62.rs\:
- \deep_preset_has_topics\
- \ un_preset_has_fun_report\
- \	opics_preset_produces_topics\

These tests asserted that \ opics\/\ un\ fields are \Some\, but the
enrichers require real source files to produce output. With mock
\ExportData\, they return \None\. Changed assertions to verify the
pipeline completes successfully and derived metrics are present.

### 3. Nix build
Same test failures propagating through Nix — fixed by the test changes
above.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stage calamares overlay in /root to avoid package conflict

archiso copies iso/airootfs/ into the work rootfs BEFORE running pacstrap,
so any file that the calamares package also provides (e.g. netinstall.yaml)
causes a 'conflicting files' transaction error.

Stage the calamares config in /root/calamares-overlay/ instead, then
copy it into /etc/calamares/ inside customize_airootfs.sh which runs
after package installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pin TrueNAS images to specific SHA to bypass cached :latest

TrueNAS caches pulled images and doesn't re-pull :latest. Using a
specific commit SHA forces a fresh pull with all fixes included.

commit.message:
Fix: use retry.RetryOnConflict to prevent retry storm on composition status updates (#574)

When multiple controllers concurrently update composition status,
conflict
errors cause immediate requeues without backoff, leading to a retry
storm
that can overwhelm the API server (142+ retries/second observed).

This fix uses k8s.io/client-go/util/retry.RetryOnConflict which:
- Re-fetches the composition before each retry attempt
- Applies exponential backoff between retries
- Handles conflicts internally instead of propagating errors

This prevents the thundering herd effect where conflict errors cascade
through the controller-runtime work queue.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable `/agent agentic-workflows debug` handling in Issue Triage workflow (#3019)

The issue triage agent workflow could not be triggered via the `/agent
...` command path for workflow debugging. This change wires the workflow
to accept `/agent agentic-workflows debug` and gives the agent the
minimal read access/tools required to inspect workflow failures.

- **Trigger surface update**
- Added `on.slash_command` to the agentic workflow source so issue
comments with `/agent` can invoke the workflow.
  - Scoped to `issue_comment` events.

- **Workflow-debug capability enablement**
- Added `permissions.actions: read` so the agent can read
workflow/job/run metadata.
  - Enabled `tools.agentic-workflows`.
- Expanded GitHub MCP toolsets to include `actions` alongside existing
`issues`/`labels`.

- **Prompt behavior update**
- Added an explicit goal line instructing the agent to handle `/agent
agentic-workflows debug` by summarizing recent workflow failures in a
concise comment.

```yaml
on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]
  slash_command:
    name: agent
    events: [issue_comment]

permissions:
  issues: read
  actions: read

tools:
  github:
    toolsets: [issues, labels, actions]
  agentic-workflows: true
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for
you](https://github.com/apollographql/rover/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for
your repo.

fix: set SigLevel=Optional TrustAll for EndeavourOS repo

The archlinux/archlinux container used in CI doesn't have the
EndeavourOS signing key, causing 'unknown trust' errors on calamares
and ckbcomp packages. TrustAll bypasses key verification for this
third-party repo while still fetching packages over HTTPS.

fix(task-runner): write error result blob on task failure (#261)

When the task runner fails (clone error, Copilot timeout, etc.), write a
structured error result to the blob store so the controller picks it up
immediately instead of polling until timeout.

Error result format: {"result_type": "error", "error": true, "summary": "..."}

The controller _parse_result now handles result_type=error by returning
a ReviewResult with the error message.

Closes #252

commit.message:
[bug-fix] azd update: fix SIGKILL during binary replacement on macOS (#7012)

* fix: remove binary before replacement to prevent SIGKILL on macOS

On macOS, copyFile used os.Create(dst) which truncates the running binary
in place (same inode). This corrupts memory-mapped code pages and causes
the OS to SIGKILL the process mid-update, leaving a broken binary.

Fix: on unix, always os.Remove(dst) before os.Create(dst). This creates
a new inode — the OS keeps the old inode alive for the running process
via its fd. Also simplifies the previous Linux-only ETXTBSY workaround
into a single remove-first path for all unix platforms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move inode test to unix-only file for Windows lint

syscall.Stat_t is undefined on Windows. Move
TestReplaceBinary_CreatesNewInode to manager_unix_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review — return error on remove failure, fix Linux inode test

Return error instead of log-and-continue when os.Remove fails unexpectedly.
Falling through to os.Create would truncate in place — same bug we're fixing.

Fix test flakiness on Linux: hold fd to old file to prevent tmpfs inode reuse.
Add root skip guard since root bypasses directory permission checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: expand environment variables reference (~80 vars) (#3344)

## Summary

Expand the Environment Variables section in CLAUDE.md from ~15
documented vars to ~80. This is a major documentation improvement that
addresses the core ask of #3333.

### New/expanded sections

| Section | Vars documented | Key additions |
|---------|----------------|---------------|
| Inference Configuration | 14 | BITNET_SEED, CROSSVAL_GGUF,
BITNET_FORCE_SCALAR, BITNET_TOKENIZER |
| Device & Threading | 6 | BITNET_DEVICE, BITNET_CPU_THREADS,
BITNET_MEMORY_LIMIT_GB |
| Server Configuration | 12 | Host/port, auth, batching, rate limits,
CORS |
| GPU Configuration | 7 13 | GPU_MEMORY_LIMIT, GPU_DEBUG, ENABLE_ROCM,
ENABLE_NPU |
| Validation & Strict Mode | 4 11 | All BITNET_STRICT_* vars,
MODEL_VALIDATION |
| Debug & Tracing | 0  11 | All BITNET_DEBUG_* and BITNET_TRACE_* vars |
| Cross-Validation | 0 6 | CPP_DIR, CPP_PATH, CROSSVAL_ENABLED/WEIGHTS,
PARITY |
| Test Configuration | 2 6 | FAST_TESTS, FORCE_GPU_TESTS,
GENERATE_FIXTURES |

### What's not covered yet

~45 vars remain undocumented  these are primarily:
- Internal test scaffolding vars (BITNET_EDGE_*)
- Model architecture override vars (BITNET_HIDDEN_SIZE,
BITNET_NUM_HEADS, etc.)
- Some server-specific vars (circuit breaker, backpressure, content
filtering)

These can be documented in a follow-up PR.

### Receipt
- **What changed**: CLAUDE.md Environment Variables section expanded
- **What ran locally**: N/A (docs-only change)
- **CI relied on**: Documentation gate
- **Parity evidence**: N/A
- **Docs touched**: CLAUDE.md  verified var names against codebase grep
- **Risks**: None
- **Disposition**: MERGE (A)

Partially addresses #3333.

commit.message:
Merge Experiment branch: allocation optimizations and benchmark fix

Brings in three performance improvements and one benchmark correctness fix:

1. NativeMemory.AlignedRealloc for buffer growth (NativeAllocator.cs)
   NativeBuffer.Grow() now extends in-place via AlignedRealloc instead of
   alloc + memcpy + free. Benefits BYTE_ARRAY columns with many pages.

2. ArrayPool for definition/repetition level buffers (ArrowArrayBuilder.cs)
   _defLevels and _repLevels switched from NativeBuffer<int> to
   ArrayPool<int>.Shared. Eliminates two native alloc/free calls per
   nullable column read.

3. In-place reverse scatter for nullable fixed-width columns (ArrowArrayBuilder.cs)
   BuildFixedArray<T> and BuildDenseFixedArray<T> no longer allocate a
   second scatter buffer. Dense values are scattered right-to-left within
   the existing value buffer (safe because non-null values only move
   rightward). The validity bitmap switches from NativeBuffer<byte> to
   MemoryPool<byte>.Shared (no alignment needed for bitmaps). Eliminates
   1 AlignedAlloc + 1 AlignedFree per nullable fixed-width column.

4. Fix Parquet.Net benchmark to materialize Data (RowGroupReadBenchmarks.cs)
   Previously .Data was never accessed after ReadColumnAsync, so the lazy
   scatter/unpack of definition levels was never triggered. Now touches
   .Data.Length to force UnpackDefinitions — making the comparison
   equivalent to what EW produces eagerly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: introduce BmwContext, BmwRequest, and route jump table (fixes #922)

Introduce the BMW request context abstraction and O(1) route dispatch:

BmwContext (Core) — lightweight request context created once per request.
Resolves Kestrel IFeatureCollection features exactly once (response,
connection, body pipes). Holds BmwRequest, source IP, route parameters,
and PageInfo. HttpContext remains accessible for migration compatibility.

BmwRequest (Core) — readonly struct with Method, Path, QueryString,
and pre-computed RouteKey ("METHOD /path") for jump-table lookup.

RouteHash (Host) — FNV-1a hash function operating on string and
ReadOnlySpan<char>. Deterministic, branchless inner loop.

RouteJumpTable (Host) — open-addressing hash table with linear probing.
Only indexes exact-match routes (no parameters/regex/catch-all).
Load factor ≤ 0.5 for fast probing. Rebuilt lazily when routes change.

RequestHandler updated to:
- Create BmwContext once at pipeline entry (feature resolution once)
- Use jump table as primary O(1) fast path for exact-match routes
- Fall through to pattern matching for parameterised routes
- Eliminate redundant string concatenation for route key

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ios] not calling activation callback on inactive gestures (#3986)

## Description

Gestures always call activation callback upon finalizing, regardless of
the previous state. This is intended for normal gestures, however for
manually handled gestures we don't want to call it. This PR unifies the
behaviour across platforms, GestureManager.finalize() won't call
activation callback.

## Test plan

Tested on the state manager example

---------

test: add 126 deep protocol tests for telemetry adapters (#196)

Add comprehensive test coverage for protocol correctness, cross-adapter
consistency, edge cases, timing guarantees, and field coverage across
Forza (Sled/CarDash/FM8/FH4), Codemasters Mode 1, OutGauge (LFS/BeamNG),
GT7, and NormalizedTelemetry helpers.

12 test modules:
- forza_protocol: format detection, field extraction, gear encoding, F->C
- codemasters_protocol: Mode 1 parsing, wheel speed, FFB from lateral G
- outgauge_protocol: LFS/BeamNG OutGauge, dash lights, cross-adapter match
- gt7_protocol: magic validation, packet sizes, decrypted buffer parsing
- cross_adapter_consistency: bounds checking across all adapters
- edge_cases: empty/truncated/NaN/Infinity/max values
- timing_guarantees: parse within 1ms budget
- field_coverage: all NormalizedTelemetry fields populated
- known_good_data: realistic driving scenarios
- adapter_trait_compliance: game_id, update rate, factory registry
- normalized_helpers: speed_kmh/mph, total_g, rpm_fraction, builder
- protocol_constants: documented packet sizes and constants

commit.message:
Reduce custom operator behavior in the Volatile<T> structure (#124244)

- None of the logic changed here is actually significant. The only
actual bug here is the behavior of `Module::SetDebuggerInfoBits`, and
`Module::EnableEditAndContinue` which now use interlocked operations for
situations that can theoretically race, due to the behavior of
Reflection.Emit.
- The PAL changes are to remove marking fields which are always
protected with a critical section for using volatile
- The GC changes are mostly around alloc_context_count which has
interesting behavior. It is intentionally written using racy logic which
is approximately correct. I'm hesitant to actually change the behavior
here without much more extensive testing, but we may want to consider
whether or not the increased re-order buffer lengths and whatnot in
modern CPUS make it more important to consider using atomic
increment/decrement here.

These are most of the non-rote changes extracted from #124106, for
independent review/merge.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(capture): add protocol analysis, replay validation, and timing stats (#194)

Add protocol analysis, replay validation pipeline, timing statistics,
vendor auto-detection, capture format validation, and community sharing
format to the hid-capture and openracing-capture-ids crates.

hid-capture lib additions:
- CaptureMetadata and SharedCaptureFile for versioned community sharing
- compute_timing_stats: jitter, p99, rate, std_dev from captures
- validate_capture_file / validate_shared_capture_file
- detect_vendor / detect_vendor_by_id with KnownVendor enum
- filter_by_report_id, capture_duration_us, validate_monotonic_timestamps
- to_shared_format conversion helper

openracing-capture-ids lib additions:
- DetectedVendor enum with detect_vendor_from_vid / detect_vendor_from_vid_str
- analyze_capture_timing: nanosecond-level timing stats for CapturedReport
- validate_replay_pipeline: decode success/failure stats through parsers
- vendor_label for human-readable vendor names

72 new tests across two test files covering:
- Timing analysis (uniform/variable/edge cases)
- Vendor detection (known vendors, unknown, invalid input)
- Capture format validation (VID/PID, monotonic timestamps)
- Shared capture format roundtrip (JSON, disk, metadata)
- Replay validation pipeline (single/multi-vendor, bad data)
- Filter, duration, and monotonic timestamp utilities

repository.name: OpenRacing
repository.description: Rust 1kHz force-feedback + telemetry stack for sim racing wheels. Safety-first. Cross-platform. Plugins + UI + CLI.

commit.message:
test(plugins): add 102 plugin ecosystem safety tests (#193)

Add comprehensive integration tests for the plugin ecosystem covering:

- WASM sandbox boundary enforcement (14 tests): invalid bytes rejection,
  missing exports, memory isolation, trap handling, fuel-based termination,
  crash isolation between sibling plugins
- Native plugin ABI compatibility (3 tests): version matching, mismatch
  detection, pre-release handling
- Capability model (9 tests): selective grants, deny-by-default, scoped
  network/file access, independent per-plugin capabilities
- Budget enforcement (6 tests): fuel exhaustion disabling, per-call fuel
  replenishment, memory limits, max instance caps, disabled plugin rejection
- Plugin registry lifecycle (18 tests): add/search/remove, semver
  compatibility, multiple versions, case-insensitive search, metadata
  validation, serde roundtrip
- Plugin signing and trust (16 tests): Ed25519 sign/verify roundtrip,
  tampered data rejection, wrong key rejection, trust store lifecycle,
  fail-closed mode, strict/dev/permissive verification configs
- Quarantine management (4 tests): manual quarantine/release, isolation
  between plugins, unknown plugin handling
- Manifest validation (7 tests): constraint boundary tests for safe/fast
  modes, empty field rejection
- Plugin lifecycle (5 tests): hot reload, re-enable after crash, reload
  with invalid bytes, nonexistent plugin handling

All tests follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() - uses Result return types and explicit assertions
- No heap allocation patterns that violate RT constraints
- Exercises safety-critical error paths and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: ignore push failure in ha-addon version sync (race condition fix)

pushes the version sync to main, and subsequent runs fail with

Chain connections (MST), placement preview, enhanced upgrade visuals

- Connections now chain through nearest neighbors via MST (Prim's algorithm)
- Multiple chains route through closest buildings back to factory
- Ghost placement preview shows building at cursor with hitbox circle
- Preview highlights conflicts (upgrade mode) vs valid placement
- Ghost preview recomputes MST to show where new node would chain
- All 4 building types have 3 distinct visual upgrade tiers
- Lv.2: extra details, multiple effects, brighter palette
- Lv.3: epic visuals with orbiting particles, rainbow effects, energy rings

Simplify comments: replace Javadoc blocks with inline // comments - Remove all @author, @version, @param, @return, @throws tags - Replace /** */ Javadoc blocks with concise // line comments - Strip HTML tags (

,

    ,
  • , etc.) and {@link}/{@code}/{@value} references - Remove trivial getter/setter/constructor comments entirely - Shorten field comments or remove when obvious from variable name - Remove decorative separator lines (// ---...---) from class bodies - Keep inline // comments inside method bodies unchanged - Keep comments explaining non-obvious logic (score formulas, loot calc, etc.) Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

bug where enemies slid along tile edges/grid lines.

commit.message:
feat: add image captioning task (GIT/microsoft/git-base-coco)

- Add OnnxImageCaptioningTransformer with autoregressive greedy decoding
  - Vision encoder (CLIP preprocessing, 224x224)  visual features [1, 197, 768]
  - Text decoder: token-by-token generation with BOS=[CLS], EOS=[SEP]
  - Uses BertTokenizer from Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers for decoding
- Add OnnxImageCaptioningOptions, Estimator, DataView, Cursor
- Add PreprocessorConfig.GIT preset (CLIP normalization, 224x224)
- Add MLContext.Transforms.OnnxImageCaptioning() extension
- Add 8 integration tests (all passing)
- Add ImageCaptioning sample project
- Export script: vision encoder + text decoder as separate ONNX files
- 164 tests passing (105 inference + 45 core + 14 tokenizer), 1 skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
